purpose: Compact agent bootstrap map for working on Vai without rediscovering the same tools, tests, and throttling rules every session.
principles: Start with AGENTS.md and /api/agent/introspect before changing code.
Prefer narrow, decision-relevant tests before expensive full-suite runs.
Use read-only delegation for independent exploration; keep write scopes disjoint.
Make tool use visible in progress traces and backlog evidence.
One heavy GPU, browser, build, or disk task at a time on this Windows machine.
bootstrapOrder: run the narrow tests for the files touched

commands.use: Print the current agent tool map, live runtime availability, and safest first commands.
Check whether the runtime is healthy before starting or restarting anything.
Read Vai's live self-description: model roster, Council pipeline, channels, key paths, docs, and tooling map.
Probe the deterministic engine without depending on stale runtime transport state.
Exercise the real user path through the live runtime and WebSocket stream.
Verify Council topic routing, delegation, and local model seating.
Verify Council routing, depth caps, redraft behavior, directed reads, and cross-checks without running the full suite.
Verify ChatService routing, fallback, classifier, and turn-policy changes.
Verify local model discovery, niche seating, configured model registration, and server ChatService options.
Verify the in-chat process tree, ThinkingPanel evidence, live stream rows, and reveal behavior after UI/process payload changes.
Verify deterministic response routes, regex lanes, and fact-shim behavior.
Focused chat/build/sandbox quality gate; use flags like --live, --unit-wide, --e2e-quick, or --promptfoo only when the change needs them.
Typecheck runtime changes including routes, server wiring, and Council roster code.
Typecheck core changes when local dependency type packages are present.
Capture the live process UI as a human-visible timeline, screenshots, and video.
Use Playwright eyes for desktop UI regressions after visible process or styling changes.
Inspect the self-improvement loop state before starting, stopping, or recovering it.

delegation.localCodex: Use subagents only for explicitly delegated, parallel, bounded work. Prefer explorers for read-only maps and workers only for disjoint file ownership.
delegation.vaiCouncil: Balanced turns should ask a bounded best-fit specialist panel; deep turns may use the full roster; quick turns skip Council.
delegation.visibility: When a delegate/tool runs, surface who was asked, why, and what evidence it produced.

externalResearchRefs.name: OpenAI tools guide
externalResearchRefs.lesson: Keep tools dynamically discoverable and load only relevant tool surfaces to reduce context cost.

externalResearchRefs.name: OpenAI Codex subagents
externalResearchRefs.lesson: Delegate parallel sidecar work, then validate and integrate locally.

externalResearchRefs.name: MCP tools specification
externalResearchRefs.lesson: Tools need clear metadata, visible invocation, and human approval for risky operations.

externalResearchRefs.name: Browserbase introduction
externalResearchRefs.lesson: Remote browser agents are useful for isolated E2E and data extraction; local Playwright remains the default first visual gate.
